tasks.title: Create independent shared-contract regression suites
tasks.summary: Verify domain, model-manifest, archive, and fake-adapter contracts independently from their implementing lane.
tasks.objective: Prevent parallel application and ML work from drifting away from accepted shared interfaces.
tasks.context: Contract fixtures are the first cross-lane dependency and require independent schema, compatibility, and behavior checks.
implementation_notes: Consume public interfaces and checked-in fixtures rather than implementation internals.
Include failures for duplicate class IDs, hash mismatch, incompatible versions, traversal paths, corrupt archives, and unsupported recognition states.
acceptance_criteria: Every shared contract has at least one positive and one negative independent test.
Manifest, archive, and domain fixtures are validated without relying on producer self-tests.
Compatibility and error outcomes match the accepted policy.
The suite runs through documented repository commands.
verification: Run the independent suite against current contracts.
Intentionally corrupt representative fixtures and confirm failures are detected.
Record covered invariants and any gaps.

tasks.title: Verify Android application flows and restart persistence
tasks.summary: Create independent Android instrumentation coverage for camera/gallery acquisition, ranked review, album behavior, edits, search/filter, transfer, and process restart.
tasks.objective: Demonstrate that the accepted user experience works on Android beyond isolated implementation tests.
tasks.context: The application batch spans platform adapters, shared state, local persistence, and six user-facing screens.
implementation_notes: Use deterministic test data and fake inference for application-only paths, then repeat a critical path with the production adapter.
Exercise system picker/camera/document boundaries using supported instrumentation techniques without weakening permissions.
acceptance_criteria: Camera and gallery flows each reach explicit confirmation and create exactly one durable entry.
Favorites, notes, identity edits, search/filter, and deletion persist correctly across restart.
Export/import round-trips through the user flow and invalid archives never enable confirmation.
Canceled, denied, malformed, low-storage, and missing-image states are recoverable and do not create orphan records.
verification: Run the suite on an emulator and at least one supported physical device.
Force-stop/relaunch or recreate process state at defined checkpoints.
Inspect runtime permissions and private storage behavior.
proof_required: Screenshots or recording of critical flows

tasks.title: Independently reproduce ML accuracy, equivalence, and device gates
tasks.summary: Re-run held-out/per-class evaluation, PyTorch/ONNX/Android ranking equivalence, and the accepted device latency/size/memory protocol for the release candidate.
tasks.objective: Ensure model acceptance is based on independently reproducible evidence rather than producer self-report.
tasks.context: The accepted model gates are at least 80% held-out top-five accuracy and median inference below two seconds on the selected device, within accepted budgets.
outputs: Independent aggregate and per-class evaluation report
implementation_notes: Verify hashes of code, environment, splits, model, manifest, and class map before running.
Do not tune or replace artifacts during independent verification; failures return to the owning task.
Record thermal/power state and raw timing samples.
acceptance_criteria: Independent held-out top-five accuracy is at least 80% and includes every supported class with sample counts.
Python PyTorch, Python ONNX Runtime, and Android ONNX Runtime rankings satisfy the accepted equivalence policy.
Median device inference is below two seconds under the accepted protocol.
All accepted size and peak-memory budgets pass.
The final gate decision references immutable artifact and input hashes.
verification: Run all evaluation and equivalence commands from a clean environment.
Run the full benchmark protocol on the named physical device.
Cross-check package and installed-size measurements using independent tools.
non_goals: Selecting a different model during verification
Marketing claims beyond the accepted test scope

tasks.title: Red-team photo privacy and archive integrity
tasks.summary: Challenge metadata removal, private storage, archive path handling, checksums, resource limits, conflicts, and atomic import behavior.
tasks.objective: Prove that local-only storage and manual transfer do not leak unnecessary metadata or corrupt the album under hostile inputs.
tasks.context: Privacy and archive safety are explicit merged boundaries, and invalid imports must cause no partial mutation.
implementation_notes: Inspect actual saved and exported image bytes with an independent metadata reader.
Test traversal, absolute paths, symlinks if applicable, duplicate names/IDs, checksum mismatch, excessive counts/sizes, truncation, and interruption.
Snapshot album/file state before each failed import and compare afterward.
acceptance_criteria: Managed and exported copies contain no unnecessary location/device metadata.
Private files are not exposed through unintended public paths or logs.
All malicious, corrupt, incompatible, and resource-exhaustion fixtures fail before durable mutation.
Injected failures leave album metadata and managed files in a consistent state with temporary data cleaned.
verification: Run adversarial archive corpus and resource-limit cases.
Run failure injection at staging and commit boundaries and compare state snapshots.

tasks.title: Verify the complete application in airplane mode
tasks.summary: Exercise install-to-album, recognition, correction, persistence, search/filter, export, and import with network access disabled.
tasks.objective: Prove the product's central subscription-free and remote-service-free promise as a complete user journey.
tasks.context: Recognition and collection behavior must work fully offline with a bundled model and local data.
inputs: Representative supported and unsupported image fixtures
implementation_notes: Enable airplane mode before first application launch after installation where practical.
Observe network requests or failures with platform tooling; the app must not require connectivity to proceed.
Include both ranked-candidate confirmation and manual correction.
acceptance_criteria: A fresh installation launches and exposes the bundled model/catalog without network access.
Camera and gallery images can be recognized, confirmed/corrected, and saved offline.
Album edits, restart persistence, search/filter, export, and import work offline.
No recognition, account, telemetry, or remote configuration request is required or retried in a user-disruptive way.
verification: Run the documented journey on a supported physical device in airplane mode.
Inspect system/network logs for unexpected requests.
Restart the device/application and repeat a saved-entry lookup.

tasks.title: Publish and verify a Google Play closed-testing build
tasks.summary: Prepare signed release configuration and compliance materials, publish to a closed track, and verify install/update behavior with selected testers.
tasks.objective: Meet the accepted MVP distribution boundary without expanding into a public production launch.
tasks.context: Google Play closed testing is required; public release remains post-MVP. Credentials and approvals remain human-managed.
inputs: Human-managed signing and Play Console access
outputs: Closed-track release checklist and compliance records
Tester install/update evidence and rollback notes
implementation_notes: Never commit keystores, passwords, service credentials, or Play Console secrets.
Pin and record application ID, versioning, artifact hashes, model/catalog version, notices, and release notes.
Stop if store declarations conflict with the actual local-only behavior or required licenses.
acceptance_criteria: All prerequisite verification gates pass and reference the exact release artifact/model hashes.
A signed release artifact satisfying accepted size budgets is uploaded to a Google Play closed-testing track.
At least one authorized tester installs the build through Play and verifies an update or documents why first-release update proof is deferred.
Store disclosures accurately describe local photos/data, offline inference, permissions, and absence of accounts/telemetry.
No secret material is present in Git or logs.
verification: Rebuild the release artifact from a clean checkout using documented secret injection.
Compare uploaded artifact and model hashes with approved evidence.
Perform closed-track install and update checks.
Run a secret scan and review Play declarations/notices.
tasks.handoff_notes: After this task is accepted, MVP implementation proof exists; public release still requires a separately approved post-MVP decision.
